content.problemStatement:
A **weighted directed graph** consists of a set VVV of **vertices** and a collection EEE of **edges**. Each edge eŌłłEe \in EeŌłłE is an ordered triple e=(x,y,w)e = (x, y, w)e=(x,y,w) where xŌłłVx \in VxŌłłV and yŌłłVy \in VyŌłłV are vertices, called the **start** and **end** vertices of eee, respectively, and www is a number called the **weight** of eee.

A **walk** from vertex sss to vertex ttt is an alternating sequence of vertices and edges (v1,e1,v2,e2,ŌĆ”,ekŌłÆ1,vk)(v_1, e_1, v_2, e_2, \ldots, e_{k-1}, v_k)(v1ŌĆŗ,e1ŌĆŗ,v2ŌĆŗ,e2ŌĆŗ,ŌĆ”,ekŌłÆ1ŌĆŗ,vkŌĆŗ) (there are kkk vertices and kŌłÆ1k - 1kŌłÆ1 edges in this sequence) satisfying the following:


kŌēź1k \ge 1kŌēź1;
v1=sv_1 = sv1ŌĆŗ=s;
vk=tv_k = tvkŌĆŗ=t;
The start vertex of eie_ieiŌĆŗ is viv_iviŌĆŗ (for each iii from 111 to kŌłÆ1k - 1kŌłÆ1);
The end vertex of eie_ieiŌĆŗ is vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1ŌĆŗ (for each iii from 111 to kŌłÆ1k - 1kŌłÆ1).
You are given a special weighted directed graph (V,E)(V, E)(V,E). The weights of the edges in this graph are **complex numbers**, that is, numbers of the form a+bia + bia+bi. Each complex weight a+bia + bia+bi satisfies the following:


aaa and bbb are integers;
a2+b2<5a^2 + b^2 < 5a2+b2<5.
We say that a walk from sss to ttt is **real** if the **product** of the weights of the edges in it is a real number. We call this product the **cost** of the walk.

Given two vertices sss and ttt, is there a **real** walk from sss to ttt? If so, what is the minimum possible cost of any real walk from sss to ttt? Note that:


It may be possible for the costs of the real walks to be arbitrarily small; you must also detect if this is the case.
If aaa and bbb are integers for every edge weight a+bia + bia+bi, then it can be shown that the cost of any real walk is an integer. In particular, the minimum possible cost is an integer.
The minimum possible cost may be very large, so only output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
The product of two complex numbers a+bia + bia+bi and aŌĆ▓+bŌĆ▓ia' + b'iaŌĆ▓+bŌĆ▓i is defined by multiplying them via the distributive property and then reducing i2=ŌłÆ1i^2 = -1i2=ŌłÆ1. For example:

(3+4i)Ōŗģ(4ŌłÆ7i)=3Ōŗģ4ŌłÆ3Ōŗģ7i+4iŌŗģ4ŌłÆ4iŌŗģ7i=12ŌłÆ21i+16iŌłÆ28i2=12ŌłÆ21i+16iŌłÆ28(ŌłÆ1)=40ŌłÆ5i.\begin{align*}
(3 + 4i)\cdot (4 - 7i)
&= 3\cdot 4 - 3\cdot 7i + 4i\cdot 4 - 4i\cdot 7i \\
&= 12 - 21i + 16i - 28i^2 \\
&= 12 - 21i + 16i - 28(-1) \\
&= 40 - 5i. 
\end{align*}(3+4i)Ōŗģ(4ŌłÆ7i)ŌĆŗ=3Ōŗģ4ŌłÆ3Ōŗģ7i+4iŌŗģ4ŌłÆ4iŌŗģ7i=12ŌłÆ21i+16iŌłÆ28i2=12ŌłÆ21i+16iŌłÆ28(ŌłÆ1)=40ŌłÆ5i.ŌĆŗWe say that a complex number a+bia + bia+bi is real if b=0b = 0b=0. In this case, we consider a+bia + bia+bi equal to the real number aaa.

For real numbers m>0m > 0m>0 and aaa, we define (aŌĆŖmodŌĆŖm)(a \bmod m)(amodm) as the unique real number aŌĆ▓a'aŌĆ▓ such that 0ŌēżaŌĆ▓<m0 \le a' < m0ŌēżaŌĆ▓<m and aŌłÆaŌĆ▓a - a'aŌłÆaŌĆ▓ is divisible by mmm.


Input Format
The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases. The following lines describe the test cases.


The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers ŌłŻVŌłŻ|V|ŌłŻVŌłŻ and ŌłŻEŌłŻ|E|ŌłŻEŌłŻ, where ŌłŻVŌłŻ|V|ŌłŻVŌłŻ denotes the number of vertices in VVV and ŌłŻEŌłŻ|E|ŌłŻEŌłŻ denotes the number of edges in EEE. For simplicity, the vertices are numbered 111 to ŌłŻVŌłŻ|V|ŌłŻVŌłŻ.


The second line contains two space-separated integers sss and ttt representing two vertices (which may be equal).


The next ŌłŻEŌłŻ|E|ŌłŻEŌłŻ lines describe the edges. Each of the next ŌłŻEŌłŻ|E|ŌłŻEŌłŻ lines contains four space-separated integers xxx, yyy, aaa and bbb denoting that (x,y,a+bi)(x, y, a + bi)(x,y,a+bi) is an edge.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing:



An integer denoting the minimum possible cost of any real walk from sss to ttt. (This number must be reduced modulo 998244353998244353998244353.)
The string `NEGATIVE INFINITY` if the costs of the real walks are arbitrarily small.
The string `NEGATIVE` if there is no real walk from sss to ttt.
constraints: The sum of the ŌłŻVŌłŻ|V|ŌłŻVŌłŻ in a single file is Ōēż3Ōŗģ105\le 3\cdot 10^5Ōēż3Ōŗģ105
The sum of the ŌłŻEŌłŻ|E|ŌłŻEŌłŻ in a single file is Ōēż3Ōŗģ105\le 3\cdot 10^5Ōēż3Ōŗģ105
There may be edges with equal start and end vertices.
There may be multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
